sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SkyWest Q2 profit falls on higher fuel costs
feed.summary: SkyWest's Q2 profit dropped due to higher fuel costs despite a 7% increase in revenue. The company approved a $250 million stock repurchase increase and plans to expand its E175 aircraft fleet, with 11 new E175s secured for American Airlines. Analysts currently rate SkyWest as a "buy," with a median 12-month price target 20.2% above its recent closing price.

feed.title: VanEck Semiconductor ETF Options Spot-On: On July 23rd, 220.06K Contracts Were Traded, With 2.34 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) saw significant options trading activity on July 23rd, with 220.06K contracts changing hands, 2.34 million remaining as open interest, and a put/call ratio of 0.69. This high trading volume indicates considerable investor attention and speculative interest in the semiconductor sector.

feed.title: SLB Stock Surges After Impressive Earnings, Revenue Beat
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) stock surged 9.4% after reporting strong second-quarter earnings of 55 cents per share and $8.97 billion in revenue, with a 5% revenue increase year-over-year. The stock has gained 35% year-to-date and is seen bullishly by analysts. Despite some technical resistance from its 80-day moving average, the company's strong performance and analyst sentiment indicate continued investor confidence.

feed.title: F5 (FFIV) Expanded Insight For BIG IP, Is The Stock Trading At A Discount?
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) has expanded its Insight for ADSP platform with new fleet management tools for BIG-IP, aiming to enhance security workflows amidst accelerating AI vulnerabilities. While F5's share price has seen strong momentum with recent AI-focused updates and a 28.01% 90-day return, valuation narratives are mixed, with some suggesting a slight undervaluation around $409 per share and others identifying a potential discount of 18.8% based on a DCF model, despite a P/E ratio higher than a fair estimate. Investors are encouraged to consider both benefits and risks before forming their own view on the stock.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Joins SoftBank’s $40 Billion AI Loan Deal
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase is participating in a significant $40 billion bridge loan for SoftBank, aimed at supporting its artificial intelligence investments, including potential involvement with an OpenAI IPO. This deal highlights JPMorgan's central role in large-scale technology financing and could impact its future deal flow and fee-related revenue in the AI sector. Investors are advised to consider the bank's capability to secure similar mandates, although recent insider selling is noted as a risk.

feed.title: Sunoco LP stock hits all-time high at 76.46 USD
feed.summary: Sunoco LP (SUN) has reached an all-time high stock price of $76.46, reflecting a 42.52% increase over the past year and a 46.8% year

feed.title: Nevada regulators approve key steps in MGM, Caesars take-private deals
feed.summary: Nevada gaming regulators have approved key steps for the take-private transactions of MGM Resorts International and Caesars Entertainment. This approval allows both companies to raise capital and moves forward Caesars' $17.6 billion acquisition by Fertitta Entertainment, with licensing approved for Fertitta Entertainment's CFO and General Counsel. Questions remain regarding MGM's Macau operations and Osaka project under a potential Diller-led private ownership, while Caesars prepares for integrating Golden Nugget's operations and addresses past AML compliance issues.

feed.title: Welltower stock hits all-time high at 250.14 USD
feed.summary: Welltower Inc. (NYSE: WELL) stock achieved a new all-time high of $250.14, representing a 54.66% increase over the last year. This surge is supported by strong Q1 2026 earnings, where EPS and revenue exceeded expectations. The company also announced plans for a C$750 million bond sale and received optimistic analyst ratings from Mizuho and Barclays.

feed.title: These Analysts Raise Their Forecasts On RTX Following Better-Than-Expected Q2 Results
feed.summary: RTX Corp. reported strong second-quarter results, surpassing analyst estimates for both earnings and sales. Following this performance and a raised full-year financial outlook, two analysts, David Strauss of Wells Fargo and Charles Minervino of Susquehanna, increased their price targets for RTX stock.

feed.title: Citi Drops Rand Bet After South Africa's Surprise Rate Hold
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. has closed its bullish position on the South African rand against the euro after the South African Reserve Bank unexpectedly kept interest rates unchanged. This decision contradicted analysts' expectations and severely impacted Citi's underlying assumptions, resulting in a loss of 2.13% on a $22 million notional position. Citi now expects the rand to remain "fragile" due to rising energy costs and the central bank's actions potentially affecting investor confidence in government bonds.

feed.title: Strattec Security (STRT): Insider Buy Signals Undemanding Valuation
feed.summary: Riverwater Micro Opportunities Strategy has added Strattec Security Corporation (NASDAQ: STRT) to its portfolio, noting that new management is driving margin expansion and customer diversification. The fund views recent significant stock purchases by the CEO and CFO as a strong signal of undemanding valuation. Despite this, Strattec Security is not among the 40 most popular stocks among hedge funds, and the article suggests that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with less downside risk.

feed.title: Travelers (TRV) insider William H. Heyman plans Rule 144 sale of 5,206 shares
feed.summary: Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) insider William H. Heyman plans to sell 5,206 shares of common stock under Rule 144 through Fidelity Brokerage Services on or after July 24, 2026. These shares were acquired as restricted stock vesting on February 21, 2024, as compensation. The aggregate market value of the planned sale is $1,978,280.00, and Heyman has reported other Travelers stock sales in the past three months.

feed.title: Union Pacific picks up Canadian National's support for its plan to acquire Norfolk Southern railroad
feed.summary: Union Pacific has secured Canadian National's support for its proposed $85 billion acquisition of Norfolk Southern after offering concessions. This deal would create the nation's first transcontinental railroad, but faces opposition from BNSF and CPKC due to concerns about market concentration. The U.S. Surface Transportation Board is reviewing the merger, which also has divided shippers.

feed.title: Needham sees AMD-Anthropic deal raising odds of more Riot Platforms leases
feed.summary: Investment bank Needham maintains a "Buy" rating and $28.50 price target for Riot Platforms following AMD's announcement of a deal with Anthropic. AMD's commitment to Anthropic, including using its AI chips and a potential $5 billion investment, is expected to increase the likelihood of AMD exercising its expansion rights at Riot's Rockdale data center or directing customers to Riot. Needham highlights Riot's Corsicana site as a significant asset for AI/HPC tenants, valuing its upside potential based on projected bitcoin prices and data center growth.

feed.title: SLM Q2 Earnings, Revenue Fall
feed.summary: SLM Corporation (Sallie Mae) reported a decline in its second-quarter earnings and revenue. The company's adjusted net income decreased to $0.80 per share, falling short of analyst expectations, primarily due to higher-than-anticipated charge-offs and a larger reduction in its managed loan portfolio. Despite these challenges, SLM reaffirmed its full-year earnings guidance, citing strong fundamentals in the student loan market and disciplined expense management.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace stock hits all-time high at 290.9 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) has reached an all-time stock high of $290.90, reflecting a 54% total return over the past year and a 40% year-to-date gain. This surge is supported by positive analyst revisions and strong Q1 2026 financial results, which surpassed revenue and earnings expectations. Despite a current InvestingPro valuation indicating the stock is overvalued, analysts have raised price targets, underscoring confidence in the company's performance in the aerospace and gas turbine sectors.

feed.title: Citigroup (C) Announces Dividend Increase of 11.7%
feed.summary: Citigroup (C) has announced an 11.7% increase in its dividend payout to $0.67, signaling confidence in its financial health. While the company's GF Score™ of 74/100 indicates solid performance, particularly in growth (8/10), its financial strength is rated low (3/10), and insiders have shown net selling activity. Investors are advised to perform due diligence despite the positive dividend news.

feed.title: US business activity perks up in July, helped by World Cup, but trend may not last, S&P Global says
feed.summary: US business activity showed an unexpected acceleration in July, driven by increased services sector activity partly due to spending around the FIFA World Cup and Independence Day. However, S&P Global warns that this trend may be short-lived, as manufacturing growth eased and intensifying conflict in the Iran region is causing renewed supply chain delays and rising energy prices, posing risks to the economic outlook.

feed.title: Clorox stock trades steadily as margins recover and household demand supports guidance
feed.summary: Clorox's stock performance reflects a gradual recovery of margins following a cyberattack in late 2023, with recent results showing improved profitability and stable demand for its household brands. The company is focusing on cash generation, disciplined capital allocation, and maintaining its dividend, with its cleaning products anchoring a diversified portfolio. Its strong brand recognition and innovation in core categories help sustain market share and pricing power.

feed.title: Gold stuck in neutral as S&P Global highlights healthy US PMI activity
feed.summary: Gold prices are holding above $4,000 an ounce but lack sustained bullish momentum due to a resilient U.S. economy. S&P Global's flash Composite PMI reached an eight-month high in July, driven by strong services sector growth, while manufacturing also expanded despite a slight slowdown. This mixed economic data has had little immediate impact on the gold market.

feed.title: Stanley Black & Decker hit with class action over alleged tariff pricing scheme
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it unlawfully profited from tariffs imposed by the federal government. The lawsuit claims the company raised prices on Dewalt-branded products to offset tariff costs, and now stands to recover these tariff payments twice after the tariffs were ruled unauthorized. The plaintiff, Or Rothschild, seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for compensatory and punitive damages.

feed.title: DLR Stock Soars After Huge Q2 Beat And Guidance Hike
feed.summary: Digital Realty Trust Inc. (DLR) stock surged over 12% after beating Q2 earnings expectations with core FFO of $2.65 against an estimated $1.98 and revenue of $1.9B compared to a $1.66B consensus. The company also raised its 2026 core FFO guidance to $8.15–$8.20 per share, surpassing the $8.03 consensus, signaling stronger cash flow and solidifying its position in the AI and hyperscale data-center market. Analyst upgrades from Guggenheim and BTIG further reinforced market confidence in DLR's growth prospects, despite potential cost risks from a Trump administration power pledge.

feed.title: Why Costco keeps gas prices so low—it's 'straight out of the retail playbook,' expert says
feed.summary: Costco maintains low gas prices as a strategic move to encourage members to visit warehouses more frequently, shop inside, and renew their memberships. This tactic has led to record demand for Costco's gasoline, with CEO Ron Vachris noting double-digit growth in gallons sold. Financial planner Lucas Bucl advises members to be aware of this retail strategy and create shopping lists to avoid impulse purchases that could negate fuel savings.

feed.title: Cardinal Health leans into CGMs, urology supplies
feed.summary: Cardinal Health is strategically expanding its presence in the continuous glucose monitors (CGMs) and urology supplies markets through key acquisitions, aiming for long-term growth. The company recently announced plans to acquire AdaptHealth's diabetes business for $235 million and Strive Medical for $125 million, building on a previous $1.1 billion acquisition of Advanced Diabetes Supply Group. These moves are designed to scale their offerings in product categories that align with their distribution model and patient experience focus, even as Medicare's competitive bidding program approaches for these supplies.

feed.title: Axon (AXON) Stock Looks Expensive On Cash Flow But Cheap On Sales
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise (AXON) presents a mixed valuation picture: its intrinsic value based on Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) suggests it's overvalued by 15.4%, yet its Price-to-Sales (P/S) multiple indicates it could be undervalued compared to its tailored fair ratio. This discrepancy arises because investors are weighing future cash flow expectations against current sales growth in a fast-growing, subscription-heavy business model. Ultimately, the article concludes that despite a supportive sales multiple, broader valuation checks are weak, meaning Axon is not a clear bargain, and its future performance depends on converting opportunities into sustained cash generation.

feed.title: Citi Drops Rand Bet After South Africa’s Surprise Rate Hold
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. has closed its bullish position on the South African rand against the euro after the South African central bank unexpectedly kept interest rates unchanged, leading to a "fragile" outlook for the currency. The investment bank's global head of emerging markets strategy, Luis Costa, stated the move resulted in a 2.13% loss on a $22 million notional position. This decision follows Citi's prediction that the rand would remain weak after the central bank's surprise rate hold.

feed.title: STT (STT) insider files to sell 9,758 shares worth $1.8M on NYSE
feed.summary: Wei-Chung Bradford Hu, an insider at STT (State Street Corp), has filed a Form 144 notice indicating a planned sale of 9,758 common shares valued at approximately $1.8 million. The sale is anticipated to occur on or after July 24, 2026, through Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC. The filing also details prior share sales and restricted stock vesting awards received by the insider.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Q2 Revenue Rises 5.7% to $5.3B in Preliminary Results; CFO to Depart
feed.summary: GE HealthCare reported preliminary Q2 2026 revenue of $5.295 billion, a 5.7% year-over-year increase, with organic revenue growth of 3.5%. The company reaffirmed its full-year guidance and announced that CFO Jay Saccaro will depart on August 14, with George Newcomb named interim CFO. GEHC shares rose slightly following the announcement, suggesting investors were reassured by the reaffirmed guidance despite the CFO transition.

feed.title: Sanofi abandons eczema drug approval efforts in another blow to OX40 class
feed.summary: Sanofi has decided to discontinue the development of its immunology therapy, amlitelimab, for atopic dermatitis (AD), citing a lack of meaningful differentiation from existing treatments. This decision is a setback for Sanofi, which had hoped amlitelimab would succeed its blockbuster drug Dupixent, and further highlights challenges for the broader OX40 drug class, especially after concerns surfaced about Kaposi sarcoma in patients treated with a similar drug, rocatinlimab. Despite this, Sanofi will continue the Phase II study of amlitelimab for celiac disease, with results anticipated in the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Tesla Short Sellers Gain $9 Billion as Shares Tumble
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. stock recently dropped significantly after reporting weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings, leading to substantial gains for short sellers. Short sellers pocketed $4.3 billion in mark-to-market gains from a single day's selloff, and their year-to-date unrealized gains reached $9.08 billion for 2026, making Tesla the most shorted stock among the Magnificent Seven. The company's high valuation and investments in future technologies like AI and robotics are raising questions about its growth prospects.

feed.title: Amgen submits new data to challenge FDA's proposal to withdraw Tavneos
feed.summary: Amgen has submitted new data to the FDA to challenge the proposed withdrawal of its rare-disease drug Tavneos from the U.S. market. The company argues that over 70 real-world studies involving more than 2,200 patients support the drug's effectiveness and safety, despite the FDA's earlier concerns regarding lack of proven effectiveness and false statements. This move comes after the FDA's April proposal to withdraw the drug and a recommendation from Europe's drug regulator to revoke its approval due to unreliable study data.

feed.title: Above the Noise: Look beyond AI pullback
feed.summary: Despite recent pullbacks in AI-related stocks, the author believes this is a recalibration of expectations rather than a fundamental breakdown, as strong earnings suggest the growth story is intact. A broader market rally is emerging, which historically supports longer market advances. Investors should also consider the potential implications of the policy outlook post-midterms on various market aspects like taxes and regulation.

feed.title: S&P Global July US Flash Manufacturing PMI 53.8 Vs. 54.4 Estimate; 53.9 Prior
feed.summary: S&P Global reported that the July US Flash Manufacturing PMI came in at 53.8, falling short of the estimated 54.4 and the prior figure of 53.9. This data was published on July 24, 2026, at 09:46 am EDT. The article presents the latest manufacturing PMI figures from S&P Global, indicating a slight dip compared to expectations and the previous month.

feed.title: Fifth Third (FITB) Stock Could Be Cheap On Value But Rich On Earnings
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp's stock has seen a 124% return over three years, yet its valuation presents a mixed picture. While an Excess Returns model suggests the stock is 39.6% undervalued, traditional P/E ratios indicate it might be overvalued compared to industry averages and its fair P/E. The company's integration of Comerica and recent recognition as the U.S.'s Best Bank are positive factors, but the market also considers merger costs and risks associated with new AI initiatives.

feed.title: Fatal gene-editing trial in China raises ethics and oversight questions
feed.summary: A six-year-old girl has died in China after receiving an experimental, brain-directed CRISPR therapy, sparking significant ethical and oversight concerns. This tragic event was reported in conjunction with other biotech news, including an FDA panel narrowly voting to allow compounding pharmacies to produce certain unregulated peptides, a decision seen as a win for RFK Jr. The article also mentions Sanofi insulin pen shortages.

feed.title: SFGV ETF Profile: Dividends, Returns (BOATS:SFGV)
feed.summary: The SFGV ETF invests globally in equity securities, allocating 50-75% to US, 20-30% to other developed, and the rest to emerging markets, with allocations adjusting based on risk/return. It screens for positive shareholder yield and free cash flow yield, targeting attractively valued companies and strong-performing ETFs. The fund, issued by Empirical Finance LLC, has an expense ratio of 0.33%, a dividend yield of 2.35%, and its top holdings include iShares Core MSCI International Developed Markets ETF and Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF.

feed.title: Corteva Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.18 a Share, Payable Sept. 15 to Holders of Record Sept. 1
feed.summary: Corteva, Inc. announced its quarterly dividend will remain at $0.18 per share. The dividend is payable on September 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of September 1, 2026. This information was officially released by MT Newswires.

feed.title: TransAct Technologies Inc|Price:4.990
feed.summary: This article provides detailed company information for TransAct Technologies Inc (TACT), including its business operations in software-driven technology and printing solutions for the foodservice, casino, and POS markets. It lists key financial data, corporate details, executive information, and shareholder breakdowns. The article also highlights that the casino and gaming business is the company's largest revenue source, generating $26.87M in 2026Q1, representing 52.20% of total revenue.

feed.title: Simpson Manufacturing declares 30 cent quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Simpson Manufacturing Co., Inc. announced a regular quarterly dividend of 30 cents per share. This dividend is payable on October 22, 2026, to stockholders of record on October 1, 2026. The California-based company specializes in engineered structural connectors and building solutions for residential and commercial markets.

feed.title: This Shattuck Labs Analyst Begins Coverage On A Bullish Note; Here Are Top 3 Initiations For Friday
feed.summary: JP Morgan analyst Brian Cheng initiated coverage on Shattuck Labs Inc (NASDAQ: STTK) with an Overweight rating and a $10 price target. This bullish initiation is part of a broader review of analyst ratings, which also saw HSBC initiate coverage on Space Exploration Technologies Corp (NASDAQ: SPCX) with a Hold rating and Mizuho initiate coverage on SunocoCorp (NYSE: SUNC) with an Outperform rating. The article highlights these top analyst initiations for Friday.

feed.title: CHRW|CH Robinson Worldwide Inc|Price:201.200|
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. (CHRW) is a global logistics provider with segments including North American Surface Transportation (NAST), Global Forwarding, and All Other and Corporate. The company's major service, transportation and logistics, generated 91.32% of its revenue in Q1 2026. As of Q1 2026, 1581 institutions hold shares, with institutional shareholding increasing significantly from Q4 2025.

feed.title: Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. Revenue Breakdown – NYSE:MLM
feed.summary: Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. (NYSE: MLM) generated $7.00 billion USD in revenue last year. The East Group segment was the top performer, contributing $3.19 billion USD, while the United States was the primary regional contributor with $6.09 billion USD. This article details the revenue breakdown by segment and geography for the company.

feed.title: SoundThinking, Inc. Revenue Breakdown – NASDAQ:SSTI
feed.summary: SoundThinking, Inc. (NASDAQ: SSTI) generated $104.13 million USD in revenue last year, with its Subscription, Maintenance and Support segment being the top performer at $103.10 million USD. The majority of the company's revenue, $100.60 million USD, originated from the United States.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks CEO warns OpenAI Hugging Face breach brings browser SASE to the fore
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora commented on the recent Hugging Face breach, where OpenAI agents were involved, using the opportunity to promote Palo Alto's browser-based security solutions. Arora suggested that AI frontier labs should audit their internal infrastructure with AI models and deploy offensive and defensive AI agents to control autonomous models. He emphasized that the breach highlights the urgent need for enterprises, especially SMBs, to improve their security posture, advocating for secure browsers like Palo Alto's Prisma Access Browser.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE: Spotify Deleted 500,000 Fake Malcolm Todd Streams. A Former SEC Lawyer Says the Bots May Have Committed a Federal Crime
feed.summary: Spotify reportedly deleted over 500,000 fake streams of Malcolm Todd's song "Earrings" after a surge linked to a $3 million Kalshi prediction market. A former SEC lawyer suggests these bots may have committed a federal crime by fabricating data that influenced a regulated event contract, potentially violating the Commodity Exchange Act. The article highlights the legal gray areas surrounding artificial market activity in prediction markets and calls for explicit data integrity standards.

feed.title: TransDigm Group Inc (TDG) Revenue Breakdown: Business Segments, Regional Revenue & Profit Contribution
feed.summary: This article provides a revenue breakdown for TransDigm Group Inc (TDG), focusing on regional contributions. It indicates that as of April 6, 202X, the United States accounted for 62.68% of the company's revenue, while foreign countries contributed 37.32%. The article notes that detailed business segment data for recent fiscal years (FY2021-FY2025) has not yet been disclosed by the company.

feed.title: PYPL|PayPal Holdings Inc|Price:56.050|Chg%:+0.050
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of PayPal Holdings Inc. (PYPL), detailing its company profile, financial information, and key executives. It highlights PayPal's role as a technology platform for digital payments, connecting consumers and merchants globally, and lists its various brands and financing products. The article also includes information on institutional shareholdings and PayPal's primary revenue source.

feed.title: NextEra Energy (NEE) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: NextEra Energy reported strong Q2 2026 results with adjusted EPS growing 9.5% year-over-year to $1.15, and adjusted net earnings reaching $2,407 million. The company is positioned for long-term growth through strategic investments in renewables, storage, and transmission, and anticipates a merger with Dominion Energy to enhance shareholder value. NextEra Energy also provided an optimistic outlook, targeting over 8% adjusted EPS CAGR through 2032 and 10% dividend per share growth for 2026.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) Q4 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Lamb Weston announced its Q4 and fiscal year 2026 earnings, with net sales for the fiscal year rising 2% to $6,612 million, driven by strong North America performance. Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year reached $1,147 million, exceeding guidance, while net income was $290 million. The company provided fiscal 2027 guidance, projecting flat to 1% net sales growth, adjusted EBITDA of $1.1–$1.2 billion, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.95–$3.25.

feed.title: Merck stock steadies as Keytruda drives Q1 2026 revenue growth
feed.summary: Merck & Co.'s stock is primarily driven by its oncology blockbuster Keytruda, which continues to propel revenue growth in Q1 2026. The company is investing billions in R&D to expand its pipeline beyond Keytruda, while also maintaining a significant cash return program. Keytruda remains a top-selling cancer medicine globally, and its performance, alongside pipeline developments and capital allocation, influences Merck's market valuation.
